question: What's the state of small-model reasoning in 2025-2026?
reference_main_claims: Distillation from larger reasoning models is the dominant transfer technique.
Chain-of-thought fine-tuning materially improves <7B model accuracy on MATH.
Process-reward models outperform outcome-reward models on multi-step problems.
Self-consistency at inference time is cheaper than larger model swaps for many tasks.
Open-source small models still trail frontier closed models on hardest reasoning benchmarks.

question: How have vision transformers evolved since 2023?
reference_main_claims: Hybrid conv-attention architectures remain competitive on ImageNet at lower FLOPs.
Register tokens reduce attention-map artefacts and improve dense prediction.
MoE vision transformers scale parameters without proportional inference cost.
Better pretraining data outweighs architectural tweaks for many downstream tasks.
DINOv2-style self-supervision is now the default for representation learning.

question: What alternatives to RLHF are competitive in 2026?
reference_main_claims: DPO offers comparable alignment quality with simpler training than PPO-based RLHF.
KTO removes the need for paired preference data using a Kahneman-Tversky objective.
ORPO unifies SFT and preference optimisation in a single stage.
GRPO scales to reasoning tasks where outcome rewards exist.
Online iterative DPO closes the gap with online RL on harder benchmarks.

question: What attention variants have proven effective for long-context (128k+) language models?
reference_main_claims: Ring + flash attention enables exact long-context training without cubic memory.
Sliding-window attention with sinks preserves throughput at high context lengths.
Position-interpolation methods (YaRN, NTK-aware) extend pretrained models cheaply.
State-space hybrids (Mamba, Jamba) trade some quality for substantially lower latency.
Retrieval-augmented inference still beats pure long-context for most QA workloads.

question: How effective are diffusion-based models for code generation compared with autoregressive models?
reference_main_claims: Diffusion code models can edit anywhere in a file, not just append, enabling fill-in-middle naturally.
Diffusion models trade some pass@1 quality for stronger pass@k due to multi-sample diversity.
Inference latency is currently 2-4x worse than autoregressive code models of similar size.
Hybrid AR+diffusion approaches show promise for refactoring tasks.
Tooling and ecosystem maturity still favour autoregressive code models in practice.
